prompt: A photorealistic, striking magazine cover photo. A massive, heavy, dripping industrial fishing net is being hoisted out of dark, stormy ocean water. The net is completely overflowing with crushed, colorful plastic bottles, discarded containers, and synthetic trash. Caught in the very dead center of the crushing plastic is one single, tiny, fragile silver fish, gleaming slightly in the harsh light. Dramatic lighting, high contrast, feeling of suffocation and loss. No text, no charts, no graphics, no watermarks.
dataset_name: Ellen MacArthur Foundation - The New Plastics Economy
insight: By 2050, there will be more plastic by weight in the ocean than fish.
